title: viola hederacea
intro: Viola hederacea, also known as wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the violet family (Violaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of wood sorrel include its ability to aid digestion, reduce symptoms of colds and fever, and provide relief from skin irritations. Wood sorrel can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and its leaves can also be used in salads or as a culinary ingredient, although its medicinal properties are often utilized in herbal preparations.
